evidence.claim: README documents stack, quick start, setup checklist, scripts, and project structure
CLAUDE.md plus a 17KB STYLEGUIDE.md provide architecture and coding conventions
ESLint flat config is present, thoughtfully tuned, and passes clean; vendored ui/ and the styleguide are ignored with rationale comments
Prettier config + ignore are committed and source conforms
package-lock.json is committed and dependencies are version-pinned with carets
Repo layout is clean and conventional: src/lib by role, infra/, supabase/, .github/workflows

strengths.point: Lint config is deliberate, not boilerplate — it disables a SvelteKit rule that conflicts with the config-driven nav pattern and documents why
Commit subjects follow conventional style with meaningful intent (feat: / chore:), not 'wip'/'fix' dumps

risks.point: Documentation describes a different application than the one that exists: README's Project Structure and Auth Architecture sections reference hooks.server.ts, login/, auth/callback/, and +layout.server.ts that are absent — a reader following the docs is misled
CI does not enforce lint or tests (only type-check + build), so the committed lint/test discipline is not gated
Only two commits exist (template scaffold + one MVP commit), so there is no incremental history to demonstrate sustained discipline

up_one.condition: Run lint+test in CI and reconcile README/CLAUDE.md with the actual frontend-only build (or add the documented backend)

down_one.condition: Remove the ESLint config or stop committing the lockfile

conventional-repo-layout.claim: Top-level layout is conventional and self-explanatory: src/lib organized by role (data, utils, config, components), plus infra/, supabase/, .github/workflows, and committed config files

two-commit-history.claim: git log shows exactly two commits on the branch: a template scaffold and a single MVP feature commit
